## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)

On May 29, 1953, Edmund Hillary, a massive, highly skilled New Zealand mountaineer, and Tenzing Norgay, an incredibly resilient Nepali-Indian Sherpa, achieved absolute, monumental immortality in the history of human exploration. The two men successfully and aggressively summited Mount Everest, becoming the absolute very first confirmed individuals to stand at the massive, highest point on Earth. The massive news of their triumph was brilliantly relayed to London just in time for the highly historic coronation of Queen Elizabeth II.

On May 29, 1453, the incredibly massive, historically definitive Fall of Constantinople occurred. The massive Ottoman Army, heavily commanded by the brilliant Sultan Mehmed the Conqueror, aggressively breached the massive, ancient walls of the city after a brutal, 53-day siege. The massive capture of the capital absolutely annihilated the remnants of the ancient Byzantine (Eastern Roman) Empire, fundamentally severing the traditional, massive land trade routes between Europe and Asia and directly triggering the massive European Age of Discovery.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)

On May 29, 1987, Chaudhary Charan Singh, a massive, highly influential Indian agrarian leader and politician, passed away in New Delhi. Heavily championing the massive, vital cause of the deeply impoverished Indian peasantry, he served a brief, massive tenure as the 5th Prime Minister of India. His absolute, uncompromising dedication to rural empowerment and his massive, aggressive resistance against elite urban policies fundamentally solidified his absolute legacy as the massive "Champion of India's Peasants."

On May 29, 1906, active, massive, and highly militant organized resistance against British colonial rule heavily escalated in Maharashtra with the formal birth of the Abhinav Bharat Society (though activities and foundational meetings spanned the month). Heavily founded by the fiercely uncompromising revolutionary Vinayak Damodar Savarkar and his brother Ganesh, this massive, deeply secretive society aggressively advocated for absolute armed rebellion and political assassinations to fundamentally violently overthrow the British Raj, heavily inspiring a massive generation of radicalized nationalist youth.
